name: Roast Lamb with Potato, Onion and Pepper Gratin
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Baking potatoes, peeled - very thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
This one-dish oven meal is easy to prepare and needs only a bottle of wine and a loaf of bread to make it complete.
1. Split 1 clove garlic. Rub the bottom of a 16x10x2-inch gratin dish with the split clove of garlic.
2. Arrange potatoes over the bottom of the pan.
3. Chop together the remaining 5 garlic cloves and thyme. Season generously with salt, pepper, and 1/3 of the garlic-thyme mixture.
4. Layer sliced onions on top. Season with salt and pepper, and another 1/3 of the garlic and thyme.
5. Layer peppers on top of the onions. Season with salt and pepper, and the remaining 1/3 of the garlic and thyme.
6. Pour over wine, then olive oil.
7. Trim excess fat from the lamb. Season the meat generously with salt and pepper.
8. Place a sturdy oven rack directly on top of the gratin dish. Set the lamb on the rack so that juices will drip into the gratin.
9. Roast, uncovered, at 400°F for about 1 hour and 15 minutes for rare. Turn the lamb every 15 minutes, basting with the liquid from the dish underneath.
10. Remove the lamb from the oven and let it stand on the rack so that juices will drip into the gratin.
11. To serve, carve the lamb into thin slices and arrange on warmed dinner plates or on a serving platter alongside the vegetable gratin.
